briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 30, 2026. Today has been a day marked by global warnings of a climate crisis, alongside reports of various efforts both domestically and internationally to adapt to climate change and achieve carbon neutrality.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Warning: Accelerating Climate Crisis
sections.content: International news released today is sending serious warnings that climate change is pushing the Earth 'beyond its limits'. With March's highest temperature records being broken and the Antarctic Ocean observed to be 'boiling' [165, 166, 177], a dual threat combining climate change and the El Niño phenomenon is endangering Earth's systems. The increased global reliance on coal power due to the Middle East conflict [2] and record fossil fuel pollution [180] are raising concerns that efforts to combat climate change could be undermined. Domestically, President Lee warned that the energy issue is 'so serious it keeps him awake at night' [87, 91, 100, 101, 110, 118, 120, 122, 125, 127, 138, 150, 152, 156, 157, 159, 160, 162], urging a rapid transition to renewable energy.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: Amidst this recognition of crisis, there is active momentum domestically to accelerate the transition to renewable energy. President Lee specifically designated Jeju Island as a 'first mover' in renewable energy transition and a place capable of achieving the fastest results [98, 107, 125], leading to the complete abolition of restrictions on renewable energy generation permits on Jeju Island [99]. The Incheon Free Economic Zone Authority is supporting the installation of new and renewable energy in private homes [81, 90, 95, 103, 106, 108, 109, 111, 117, 119, 128, 132, 133, 134, 137, 140, 145, 147, 153, 154, 163], and local governments such as Gimhae City and Gyeonggi Province are also expanding support for residential solar panel installations [59, 67, 126, 131, 161]. However, criticisms that the process of making renewable energy a primary power source is 'disorganized' [115], along with a report stating that South Korea has the lowest proportion of renewable energy among OECD countries [164], indicate that we still have a long way to go.

sections.title: 🌱 Innovative Research and Technology Development for Climate Change Adaptation
sections.content: Amidst the threats of climate change, domestic research achievements for food security and environmental adaptation are gaining attention. A Chonnam National University research team has discovered key clues to enhance food productivity even under climate change conditions such as drought and heatwaves [1, 5, 14, 17], and the climate change-responsive microorganism 'H30-3' developed by the Rural Development Administration has been successfully commercialized and industrialized [3, 6, 15]. aT emphasized the need to recognize climate change as a constant and establish a 'climate-based system' for agricultural product supply and demand [7], while Hallym University, in collaboration with the Korea Climate Change Research Institute, launched a Climate AI Convergence Department to engage in technological responses [9]. These efforts represent significant steps towards human survival and sustainability in a changing climate environment.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Multifaceted Efforts to Achieve a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Diverse domestic efforts are actively underway to transition to a carbon-neutral society. Various organizations and local governments, including the Sudokwon Landfill Site Management Corporation, Wonju Regional Environmental Office, and Seongju County, are holding tree-planting events to promote carbon neutrality [25, 27, 31, 37, 39, 40, 43, 45, 47, 55, 56, 61, 65, 66, 68, 70, 76], and striving to secure carbon sinks through urban forest creation [33, 58, 63]. Furthermore, corporate efforts to achieve carbon neutrality are continuing, such as Sampyo Group's special lectures on construction material quality and carbon neutrality [32, 35, 49, 54, 62, 69, 71, 73, 75, 77, 80, 84] and Lotte Chilsung Beverage's strengthening of ESG management [34]. Meanwhile, discussions for establishing legal and institutional foundations are also active, with Representative Park Ji-hye, for example, introducing an amendment to the 'Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality' [36, 24, 78].

briefing.closing: As you have seen in today's briefing, climate change is no longer a distant future threat but a present reality. However, at the same time, innovative technological developments and comprehensive efforts to overcome this crisis are also expanding. Through sustained attention and effort, we must accelerate the transition to a carbon-neutral society. This concludes today's climate briefing.
